svg.title: CounterWorlds product architecture and learning loop
svg.desc: A six-stage observatory-style product diagram showing a teacher creating a classroom, anonymous students explaining their thinking, misconceptions becoming competing worlds, a synchronized counterworld experiment, an evidence reveal, and belief revision returning to the teacher.

g.text: Turn wrong answers into playable universes.
A classroom learning loop where explanations become worlds, worlds become experiments, and evidence changes minds.

g.aria-label: Two equally weighted competing model clusters
Belief revision returns to the teacher console

g.text: AI tutors give answers. CounterWorlds makes beliefs testable.
Turn wrong answers into playable universes.
Question • Learning objective • Canonical model • Create live classroom
Join anonymously • Choose a classroom nickname • Explain
Anonymous explanations cluster into models — identities stay hidden.
World A ↔ World B • Same controls • Predict before reveal
Which world matches real evidence? • Submit evidence
BELIEFS CHANGED • EVIDENCE MATCHED • REAL CLASSROOM RECORD
AI tutors give answers. CounterWorlds makes beliefs testable.
